# EOxElements Comprehensive Documentation

> This file is intended for AI agents.

# EOxElements

A Web Component collection of geospatial UI elements, crafted by EOX.

## Documentation, Examples

Please find [descriptions, API docs and interactive examples here](https://eox-a.github.io/EOxElements).

## Elements

- ⭕️ **Alpha** elements are in-development and may have many frequent breaking
  changes.
- 🟡 **Beta** elements are mostly polished and ready for use, but may still have
  breaking changes.
- ✅ **Stable** elements are reviewed, documented, and API complete.

## Usage

For detailed descriptions and documentation on the individual elements, please check out the READMEs in the element subfolders.

### Bundlers (Vite, Webpack, etc.)

```
npm install @eox/<element>
```

```
import "@eox/<element>"
<eox-element></eox-element>
```

### Script tag

```
<eox-element></eox-element>

<script type="module">
import "@eox/<element>" from "https://cdn.skypack.dev/@eox/<element>"
</script>
```

## Development

For detailed development instructions, including monorepo structure, coding standards, and testing procedures, please refer to the [Contributing Guide](CONTRIBUTING.md).

AI Agents working in this repository should also consult [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) for specific guidelines on maintaining documentation and ensuring compatibility with the MCP server.

## License

By contributing to EOxElements, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the repository's [LICENSE](LICENSE).

## Previous versions

The main branch of this project contains the v2 version of EOxElements. For the (legacy) v1 EOxElements, please see the [v1 branch](https://github.com/EOX-A/elements/tree/v1).


---

# API Reference & Usage

## eox-chart

Chart component based on [Vega-Lite](https://vega.github.io/vega-lite/)/[Vega-Embed](https://github.com/vega/vega-embed).
Pass a valid Vega spec as `spec` property or attribute in order to render a chart.
Optionally for transfer of chart definitions with both single and double quote as attribute, use the `spec` as base64 encoded string.

The `eox-chart` provides some default `spec` settings (merged with the provided `spec` property/attribute) and helper functionalities on top of Vega-Lite.

Default `spec` settings (see the file `src/enums/default-spec.js`):
- `width`: "container" (make the chart width responsive to the parent)
- `height`: "container" (make the chart height responsive to the parent)
- `autosize`: "fit" (automatically adjust the layout in an attempt to force the total visualization size to fit within the given width, height and padding values)
- `resize`: true (autosize layout is re-calculated on every view update)
- `padding`: 16 (the padding in pixels to add around the visualization)

These default `spec` settings can be overwritten by setting them to a differnt value in the `spec` property/attribute passed to `eox-chart`. Also, there are default
Vega-Embed options (see the file `src/enums/default-opt.js`), which can also be overwritten in the passed `opt` property/attribute.

Helper functionalities:

The `eox-chart` automatically emits mouse/pointer events from the Vega-Lite chart. See below for the emitted events.
For working with base64 encoded `spec` or `dataValues` attributes, `eox-chart` exports two helper methods: `base64EncodeSpec` (encoding), `parseSpec` (decoding).

### Attributes

- `dataValues` ({[dataSourceName: string]: InlineData} | string): Data values passed on runtime. Requires a [named data source](https://vega.github.io/vega-lite/docs/data.html#named) in the provided `spec`. Either passed as a base64 encoded string or as an object.
- `spec` (VisualizationSpec | string): [Vega-Lite spec](https://vega.github.io/vega-lite/docs/spec.html) either as an object or base64 encoded string.
- `opt` (EmbedOptions): [Vega-Embed options](https://github.com/vega/vega-embed?tab=readme-ov-file#options)
- `no-shadow` (Boolean): Renders the element without a shadow root
- `unstyled` (Boolean): Render the element without additional styles

### Properties

- `spec` (VisualizationSpec | string): [Vega-Lite spec](https://vega.github.io/vega-lite/docs/spec.html) either as an object or base64 encoded string. (Default: `undefined`)
- `opt` (EmbedOptions): [Vega-Embed options](https://github.com/vega/vega-embed?tab=readme-ov-file#options) (Default: `undefined`)
- `dataValues` ({[dataSourceName: string]: InlineData} | string): Data values passed on runtime. Requires a [named data source](https://vega.github.io/vega-lite/docs/data.html#named) in the provided `spec`. Either passed as a base64 encoded string or as an object. (Default: `undefined`)
- `noShadow` (Boolean): Renders the element without a shadow root (Default: `false`)
- `unstyled` (Boolean): Render the element without additional styles (Default: `false`)

### Events

- `pointermove:item`: Chart item is hovered. Event detail includes `event` (pointermove event) and `item` (the hovered item on the chart)
- `click:item`: Chart item is clicked. Event detail includes `event` (click event) and `item` (the clicked item on the chart)

### Examples

#### Base64EncodedSpec

Passing Vega specification as base64 encoded string. This example shows use case where spec
contains both single quote and double quote which can not be passed as HTML attribute.
EOxChart exports util function `base64EncodeSpec` so that parent application can create the base64 encoded spec.

```html
<eox-chart
  style="width: 100%; height: 400px"
  spec=""
></eox-chart>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/chart/dist/eox-chart.js";

  const eoxChart = document.querySelector("eox-chart");
</script>

```

#### DataValues

Set the data values manually after chart initialization: this example demonstrates
how data can be set independently of the `spec`. This is handy if the `spec` should
stay the same, while the data updates at a later point. For this to work,
the `spec` needs to include a named data source inside `data.name`, in this case set to "myData", and inside the
passed `dataValues` property there needs to be a key also called "myData". Thus, the
data values of `dataValues.myData` are applied to the chart independently of the `spec`.

```html
<eox-chart style="width: 100%; height: 400px"></eox-chart>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/chart/dist/eox-chart.js";

  const eoxChart = document.querySelector("eox-chart");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxChart, {
    spec: {
      $schema: "https://vega.github.io/schema/vega-lite/v5.json",
      description:
        "A simple bar chart with data passed from external property.",
      data: {
        name: "myData",
      },
      mark: {
        type: "bar",
        tooltip: true,
        fill: "#004170",
      },
      encoding: {
        x: {
          field: "a",
          type: "ordinal",
        },
        y: {
          field: "b",
          type: "quantitative",
        },
      },
    },
    dataValues: {
      myData: [
        {
          a: "A",
          b: 28,
        },
        {
          a: "B",
          b: 55,
        },
        {
          a: "C",
          b: 43,
        },
        {
          a: "D",
          b: 91,
        },
        {
          a: "E",
          b: 81,
        },
        {
          a: "F",
          b: 53,
        },
        {
          a: "G",
          b: 19,
        },
        {
          a: "H",
          b: 87,
        },
        {
          a: "I",
          b: 52,
        },
      ],
    },
  });
</script>

```

#### GeoDB

Retrieve and display data from geoDB endpoint. This example shows a Vega-Lite spec
that fetches data from a live endpoint and renders it as a line chart.

```html
<eox-chart style="width: 100%; height: 400px"></eox-chart>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/chart/dist/eox-chart.js";

  const eoxChart = document.querySelector("eox-chart");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxChart, {
    spec: {
      $schema: "https://vega.github.io/schema/vega-lite/v5.json",
      description: "A chart visualizing data fetched from a geoDB endpoint",
      data: {
        url: "https://xcube-geodb.brockmann-consult.de/gtif/f0ad1e25-98fa-4b82-9228-815ab24f5dd1/GTIF_no2_data?and=(date.gte.2022-01-01,date.lte.2022-01-05)&select=no2_ec_station_ppbv,date",
        parse: {
          no2_ec_station_ppbv: "number",
          date: "date",
        },
      },
      encoding: {
        x: {
          field: "date",
          type: "temporal",
        },
      },
      layer: [
        {
          encoding: {
            y: {
              field: "no2_ec_station_ppbv",
              type: "quantitative",
            },
          },
          layer: [
            {
              mark: "line",
            },
            {
              transform: [
                {
                  filter: {
                    param: "hover",
                    empty: false,
                  },
                },
              ],
              mark: "point",
            },
          ],
        },
        {
          mark: "rule",
          encoding: {
            opacity: {
              condition: {
                value: 0.3,
                param: "hover",
                empty: false,
              },
              value: 0,
            },
            tooltip: [
              {
                field: "no2_ec_station_ppbv",
                type: "quantitative",
              },
            ],
          },
          params: [
            {
              name: "hover",
              select: {
                type: "point",
                fields: ["date"],
                nearest: true,
                on: "pointerover",
                clear: "pointerout",
              },
            },
          ],
        },
      ],
    },
  });
</script>

```

#### Primary

Basic chart example. It sets a simple Vega-Lite spec including data values
as the `spec` property of eox-chart and renders a bar chart. In this example, the `pointermove:item`
and `click:item` events of `eox-chart` are console logged.

```html
<eox-chart style="width: 100%; height: 400px"></eox-chart>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/chart/dist/eox-chart.js";

  const eoxChart = document.querySelector("eox-chart");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxChart, {
    spec: {
      $schema: "https://vega.github.io/schema/vega-lite/v5.json",
      description: "A simple bar chart with embedded data.",
      data: {
        values: [
          {
            a: "A",
            b: 28,
          },
          {
            a: "B",
            b: 55,
          },
          {
            a: "C",
            b: 43,
          },
          {
            a: "D",
            b: 91,
          },
          {
            a: "E",
            b: 81,
          },
          {
            a: "F",
            b: 53,
          },
          {
            a: "G",
            b: 19,
          },
          {
            a: "H",
            b: 87,
          },
          {
            a: "I",
            b: 52,
          },
        ],
      },
      mark: {
        type: "bar",
        tooltip: true,
        fill: "#004170",
      },
      encoding: {
        x: {
          field: "a",
          type: "ordinal",
        },
        y: {
          field: "b",
          type: "quantitative",
        },
      },
    },
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxChart.addEventListener("pointermove:item", (e) =>
    console.log("Chart hovered! Hovered item: ", e.detail.item),
  );
  eoxChart.addEventListener("click:item", (e) =>
    console.log("Chart clicked! Clicked item: ", e.detail.item),
  );
</script>

```

---

## eox-drawtools

The `eox-drawtools` element provides a comprehensive set of drawing, editing, selection, and import tools for vector features on an `eox-map`. It supports drawing multiple feature types (Polygon, Box, Point, Circle, LineString), continuous drawing, feature modification, selection from existing layers, and import/export in various formats (GeoJSON, WKT, native OL feature).

Key features:
- Draw polygons, boxes, points, circles, and lines on the map
- Draw multiple features at once (set `multiple-features`)
- Continuous drawing mode (set `continuous`)
- Modify drawn features (set `allow-modify`)
- Select features from a specified layer (`layer-id`)
- Display a list of drawn/selected features (`show-list`)
- Edit features as GeoJSON in a text editor (`show-editor`)
- Import features via drag-and-drop or file upload (`import-features`)
- Emit drawn features in different formats (`format`: `feature`, `geojson`, `wkt`)
- Emit features in a specified projection (`projection`)
- Customizable feature styles (`featureStyles`)
- Unstyled and no-shadow variants for easy integration

Usage examples and visual demos are available in Storybook stories, including scenarios for multi-feature drawing, feature modification, selection, import/export, continuous drawing, format and projection control, and style customization.

## Methods

- `startDrawing`: Triggers starting the drawing interaction on the map.
- `stopDrawing`: Triggers stopping the drawing interaction on the map (same as pressing the Escape key).
- `discardDrawing`: Triggers discarding/stopping the drawing interaction and deleting the drawn shapes.
- `removeFeature`: Removes a feature from the drawn features.
- `removeFeatureByIndex`: Removes a feature from the drawn features by its index.

Usage: `document.querySelector("eox-drawtools").startDrawing();`

### Attributes

- `allow-modify` (boolean): Allow modifying the drawn feature(s)
- `for` (String|HTMLElement): Query selector of an `eox-map` (`String`, passed as an attribute or property)
or an `eox-map` DOM element (`HTMLElement`, passed as property)
- `continuous` (boolean): Enables the user to draw continuously one feature after another
without having to discard previous ones manually
- `feature-name` (string): The display name of drawn features, shown e.g. in the feature list.
- `feature-name-key` (null): The key of the property to display in the feature list.
- `layer-id` (string): The ID of the Vector/Vector Tile Layer that contains features to be selected
- `featureStyles` ({
     * layer?: FlatStyleLike
     * hover?: FlatStyleLike
     * click?: FlatStyleLike
     * } | null): Flat styles for the drawn/selected features
- `multiple-features` (boolean): Allow adding more than one feature at a time
- `measure` (boolean): Display area/line measurement for the drawn features.
When enabled, a "measure" property is added to each drawn feature's properties,
containing the calculated area (for Polygons and Circles) or length (for LineStrings).
This measurement is also displayed as a text label on the map using OpenLayers flat styles.
- `import-features` (boolean): Allow import features using drag-drop and upload button
- `show-editor` (boolean): Show geo-json editor for draw tool
- `show-list` (boolean): Show list of features
- `projection` (string): Projection of the emitted drawn features
- `no-shadow` (Boolean): Renders the element without a shadow root
- `format` ("feature"| "geojson" | "wkt"): The format in which the drawn features should be emitted
- `type` ("Polygon" | "Point" | "LineString" | "Circle" | "Box"): Type of the drawn feature
- `unstyled` (boolean): Render the element without additional styles

### Properties

- `continuous` (boolean): Enables the user to draw continuously one feature after another
without having to discard previous ones manually (Default: `undefined`)
- `drawnFeatures` (Array<Feature>): The array of drawn native OpenLayers features. Normally includes only one feature, until multiple feature drawing is enabled.
Pass features to be drawn on the map by setting this property. The features should be in the same projection as the map, or a projection should be specified via the `projection` property for proper transformation. (Default: `undefined`)
- `layerId` (string): The ID of the Vector/Vector Tile Layer that contains features to be selected (Default: `""`)
- `allowModify` (boolean): Allow modifying the drawn feature(s) (Default: `false`)
- `for` (String|HTMLElement): Query selector of an `eox-map` (`String`, passed as an attribute or property)
or an `eox-map` DOM element (`HTMLElement`, passed as property) (Default: `"eox-map"`)
- `currentlyDrawing` (boolean): Whether the user is currently in the process of drawing or not (Default: `false`)
- `draw` (Draw | EOxMap["selectInteractions"][string]): The current native OpenLayers `draw` interaction (Default: `null`)
- `drawLayer` (default): The current native OpenLayers draw `layer` (initialized with a `zIndex` of 100) (Default: `null`)
- `featureName` (string): The display name of drawn features, shown e.g. in the feature list. (Default: `"Feature"`)
- `featureNameKey` (null): The key of the property to display in the feature list. (Default: `null`)
- `featureStyles` ({
     * layer?: FlatStyleLike
     * hover?: FlatStyleLike
     * click?: FlatStyleLike
     * } | null): Flat styles for the drawn/selected features (Default: `null`)
- `modify` (Modify): The current native OpenLayers `modify` interaction (Default: `null`)
- `multipleFeatures` (boolean): Allow adding more than one feature at a time (Default: `false`)
- `measure` (boolean): Display area/line measurement for the drawn features.
When enabled, a "measure" property is added to each drawn feature's properties,
containing the calculated area (for Polygons and Circles) or length (for LineStrings).
This measurement is also displayed as a text label on the map using OpenLayers flat styles. (Default: `false`)
- `importFeatures` (boolean): Allow import features using drag-drop and upload button (Default: `false`)
- `showEditor` (boolean): Show geo-json editor for draw tool (Default: `false`)
- `showList` (boolean): Show list of features (Default: `false`)
- `projection` (string): Projection of the emitted drawn features (Default: `"EPSG:4326"`)
- `type` ("Polygon" | "Point" | "LineString" | "Circle" | "Box"): Type of the drawn feature (Default: `"Polygon"`)
- `format` ("feature"| "geojson" | "wkt"): The format in which the drawn features should be emitted (Default: `"feature"`)
- `unstyled` (boolean): Render the element without additional styles (Default: `false`)
- `noShadow` (Boolean): Renders the element without a shadow root (Default: `false`)

### Events

- `drawupdate`: Fires whenever features are added, modified or discarded

### Examples

#### CSSVariableOverride

Override css variable directly using styles. This example demonstrates how applying the style
`"--primary: #ffa55c; --error: #00ff00;"` to the `eox-drawtools` element changes the color of the
draw button (which uses `--primary`) and the delete/discard button (which uses `--error`).

```html
<eox-drawtools
  for="eox-map#css-var-override"
  multiple-features
  show-list
  style="--primary: #ffa55c; --error: #00ff00"
></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="css-var-override" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });
</script>

```

#### ContinuousDrawing

By setting the `continuous` attribute/property to `true`,
the user can draw continuously one polygon at a time without removing the last polygon manually.

```html
<eox-drawtools continuous for="eox-map#continuous"></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="continuous" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });
</script>

```

#### DrawType

The `type` attribute/property controls which drawing type is enabled
(defaults to `Polygon`). It supports `Polygon`, `Box`, `Point`, `Circle` and `LineString`.
Inside the Storybook Controls panel, you can try changing the type and see that the draw button
changes its shape and a different draw type is enabled.

```html
<eox-drawtools
  for="eox-map#drawTypes"
  multiple-features
  allow-modify
  type="Box"
></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="drawTypes" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });
</script>

```

#### FeaturesProjection

The `projection` attribute/property controls in which projection the drawn features are emitted
(defaults to `EPSG:3857`). It supports any projections that is registered for `eox-map`(by default `EPSG:3857`
and `EPSG:4326`, but other projections can be registered - see the corresponding stories of `eox-map`).
It does not necessarily need to match the map's view projection.
Inside the Storybook Controls panel, you can try changing the projection from `EPSG:3857` to `EPSG:4326` and see
that the `console.log` writes the emitted feature in `EPSG:4326`.

```html
<eox-drawtools
  id="feature-projection"
  for="eox-map#feature-projection-map"
  projection="EPSG:3857"
  type="Box"
></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map
  id="feature-projection-map"
  style="width: 400px; height: 300px"
></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxDrawtools.addEventListener("drawupdate", (e) => {
    console.log(
      `extent of the created feature in ${e.target?.projection}`,
      e.detail?.[0]?.getGeometry().getExtent(),
    );
  });
</script>

```

#### Formats

The `format` attribute/property controls in which format the drawn features are emitted
(defaults to `feature`). It supports `feature` (feature as emitted by OpenLayers), `geojson` (GeoJSON representation)
and `wkt` (Well-Known Text / WKT representation).
Inside the Storybook Controls panel, you can try changing the projection from `feature` to something else and see
that the `console.log` writes the emitted feature in a different format.

```html
<eox-drawtools
  id="feature-projection"
  for="eox-map#formats"
  type="Box"
  format="feature"
></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="formats" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxDrawtools, {
    multipleFeatures: true,
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxDrawtools.addEventListener("drawupdate", (e) => {
    console.log(`returned values in ${e.target?.format} format `, e.detail);
  });
</script>

```

#### ImportFeaturesWithEditor

By setting the `show-editor` attribute/property to `true`, `eox-drawtools` displays a text editor to
manually edit drawn features (displayed in `GeoJSON` format) or paste features in `GeoJSON` format;
by setting the `import-features` attribute/property, it allows users to
drag-and-drop features in `GeoJSON` format (onto the text editor) or upload (via the upload button)
feature files in `GeoJSON`, `TopoJSON` and `KML` format.

```html
<eox-drawtools
  for="eox-map#import"
  multiple-features
  import-features
  show-editor
  show-list
></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="import" style="width: 100%; height: 400px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });
</script>

```

#### Measure

By setting the `measure` attribute or property to `true`, the element displays
area/line measurement for the drawn features.
The measurement logic automatically calculates the area for polygons and circles,
and the length for line segments. These values are updated in real-time as the
geometry is modified and are displayed as text labels on the map features.
The `measure` property is also added to the properties of the emitted feature.

```html
<eox-drawtools for="eox-map#measure" measure type="Polygon"></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="measure" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxDrawtools, {
    allowModify: true,
    multipleFeatures: true,
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });
</script>

```

#### ModifyFeatures

By setting the `allow-modify` attribute or `allowModify` property,
the user can modify features after drawing.

```html
<eox-drawtools for="eox-map#modify"></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="modify" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxDrawtools, {
    multipleFeatures: true,
    allowModify: true,
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxDrawtools.addEventListener("drawupdate", (e) => {
    console.log("drawupdate", e.detail);
  });
</script>

```

#### MultiFeaturesSelect

Showcasing the combination of `multiple-features`, `show-list` and `layer-id` attributes or properties, allowing the selection of multiple features.

```html
<eox-drawtools for="eox-map#multi-select" type="Box"></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="multi-select" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxDrawtools, {
    allowModify: false,
    multipleFeatures: true,
    layerId: "regions",
    showList: true,
    featureName: "Region",
    featureNameKey: "ECO_NAME",
    featureStyles: {
      layer: {
        "fill-color": "#16A105A0",
        "stroke-color": "#16A105",
        "stroke-width": 2.5,
      },
      hover: {
        "fill-color": "#19B806A0",
        "stroke-color": "#19B806",
        "stroke-width": 2.5,
      },
    },
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Vector",
        background: "lightgrey",
        properties: {
          id: "regions",
        },
        source: {
          type: "Vector",
          url: "https://openlayers.org/data/vector/ecoregions.json",
          format: "GeoJSON",
          attributions: "Regions: @ openlayers.org",
        },
        style: {
          "stroke-color": "black",
          "stroke-width": 1,
          "fill-color": "darkgrey",
        },
      },
    ],
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxDrawtools.addEventListener("drawupdate", (e) => {
    console.log("drawUpdate:", e.detail);
  });
</script>

```

#### MultiPolygon

By setting the `multiple-features` attribute or `multipleFeatures` property to `true`,
multiple polygons can be drawn.

```html
<eox-drawtools for="eox-map#multi"></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="multi" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxDrawtools, {
    multipleFeatures: true,
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxDrawtools.addEventListener("drawupdate", (e) => {
    console.log("drawupdate", e.detail);
  });
</script>

```

#### MultiPolygonWithList

By setting the `show-list` attribute or `showList` property to `true`,
a list of the drawn features will be populated.

```html
<eox-drawtools for="eox-map#list"></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="list" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxDrawtools, {
    multipleFeatures: true,
    showList: true,
    featureName: "Polygon",
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxDrawtools.addEventListener("drawupdate", (e) => {
    console.log("drawupdate", e.detail);
  });
</script>

```

#### Primary

Stories for eox-drawtools component showcasing various configurations.
These stories provide visual representations and usage examples for different scenarios.
/
import {
PrimaryStory,
DrawTypeStory,
MultiPolygonStory,
ModifyFeaturesStory,
MultiPolygonWithListStory,
UnstyledStory,
CSSVariableOverrideStory,
ImportFeaturesWithEditorStory,
SelectFeatureStory,
MultiFeaturesSelectStory,
FeaturesProjectionStory,
FormatStory,
ContinuousDrawingStory,
MeasureStory,
} from "./index";
export default {
title: "Elements/eox-drawtools",
tags: ["autodocs"],
component: "eox-drawtools",
argTypes: {
format: {
control: { type: "select" },
options: ["geojson", "wkt", "feature"],
},
type: {
control: { type: "select" },
options: ["Polygon", "Box", "Point", "Circle", "LineString"],
},
},
};
// Exporting each individual story for the eox-drawtools component.
/**
Primary story showcasing basic usage.

```html
<eox-drawtools for="eox-map#primary" type="Polygon"></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="primary" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxDrawtools, {
    allowModify: false,
    multipleFeatures: false,
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxDrawtools.addEventListener("drawupdate", (e) => console.log(e));
</script>

```

#### SelectFeature

Setting `layer-id` attribute or `layerId` property enables selection of features on the specified layer.

```html
<eox-drawtools for="eox-map#select" type="Polygon"></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="select" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxDrawtools, {
    multipleFeatures: false,
    layerId: "regions",
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Vector",
        background: "lightgrey",
        properties: {
          id: "regions",
        },
        source: {
          type: "Vector",
          url: "https://openlayers.org/data/vector/ecoregions.json",
          format: "GeoJSON",
          attributions: "Regions: @ openlayers.org",
        },
        style: {
          "stroke-color": "black",
          "stroke-width": 1,
          "fill-color": "darkgrey",
        },
      },
    ],
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxDrawtools.addEventListener("drawupdate", (e) => {
    console.log("drawUpdate:", e.detail);
  });
</script>

```

#### Unstyled

By setting the `unstyled` attribute or property, the element has no styling applied.

```html
<eox-drawtools for="eox-map#unstyled" unstyled></eox-drawtools>
<eox-map id="unstyled" style="width: 400px; height: 300px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/drawtools/dist/eox-drawtools.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxDrawtools = document.querySelector("eox-drawtools");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxDrawtools, {
    multipleFeatures: true,
    showList: true,
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    layers: [
      {
        type: "Tile",
        source: {
          type: "OSM",
        },
      },
    ],
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxDrawtools.addEventListener("drawupdate", (e) => {
    console.log("drawupdate", e.detail);
  });
</script>

```

---

## eox-feedback

The `eox-feedback` element provides a modal dialog for collecting user feedback.
It supports screenshot capture, customizable endpoint, and flexible styling.
The feedback modal (`<eox-feedback></eox-feedback>`) can be included in the DOM
directly or via a floating button (`<eox-feedback-button></eox-feedback-button>`),
which can be positioned in any corner of the viewport.

On submit, the element sends a `POST` request with `FormData` to the configured
`endpoint`. The payload includes the feedback message (or custom form fields when
using a `schema`), the current page URL, the browser's user agent, screen resolution and optionally
a screenshot file. This makes it straightforward to connect to any backend — for
example a service that creates issues in a Git platform (GitLab, GitHub, …), sends
notifications, or stores feedback in a database.

The backend only needs to accept a `multipart/form-data` POST request, extract the
fields and the optional file attachment, and then forward them to whatever system
you use for tracking or processing feedback. Because all authentication and API
tokens live on the server side, the browser never sees sensitive credentials.
Typical backend responsibilities include input sanitization, CORS configuration to
restrict which origins may submit feedback, and mapping the received fields into the
format expected by the target system (e.g. composing an issue title and body, or
building an email).

### Attributes

- `endpoint` (string): The endpoint to send the feedback to as POST message.
- `unstyled` (string): If provided, the element will not be styled with EOX styles.

### Events

- `submit`: The feedback was submitted. The `CustomEvent.detail` contains the FormData object.
- `close`: The modal was closed.

### Examples

#### Button

Triggering of eox-feeback modal via button. The button can be positioned on the page using the `position` attribute.
Can be `top-left`, `top-right`, `bottom-left`, or `bottom-right`.

```html
<eox-feedback endpoint="/fake/endpoint"></eox-feedback>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/feedback/dist/eox-feedback.js";

  const eoxFeedback = document.querySelector("eox-feedback");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxFeedback, {
    position: "bottom-left",
    click: (e) => console.log("Feedback button clicked", e),
  });
</script>

```

#### CustomForm

Custom form schema for eox-feedback.
By passing a JSON schema to the `schema` property, the form can be fully customized.
This functionality is powered by [eox-jsonform](https://github.com/EOX-A/EOxElements/tree/main/elements/jsonform).
Fields can be hidden by setting `options: { hidden: true }` in the schema property.
Values can be passed programmatically by setting the `default` property in the schema.

```html
<eox-feedback
  endpoint="https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts"
  style="position: relative !important; height: 400px"
></eox-feedback>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/feedback/dist/eox-feedback.js";

  import "@eox/jsonform";

  const eoxFeedback = document.querySelector("eox-feedback");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxFeedback, {
    schema: {
      type: "object",
      properties: {
        email: {
          type: "string",
          format: "email",
          title: "Email",
          description: "Your email address",
          options: {
            inputAttributes: {
              placeholder: "Include your email address (optional).",
            },
          },
        },
        message: {
          type: "string",
          format: "textarea",
          title: "Message",
          description: "Your feedback",
          options: {
            inputAttributes: {
              placeholder: "Type your feedback here!",
            },
          },
          minLength: 1,
        },
        hidden_field: {
          type: "string",
          options: {
            hidden: true,
          },
          default: "some-hidden-value",
        },
      },
      required: ["message"],
    },
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxFeedback.addEventListener("submit", (e) =>
    console.log("Feedback submitted", e.detail),
  );
  eoxFeedback.addEventListener("close", () => console.log("Feedback closed"));
</script>

```

#### Primary

Stories for eox-feedback component showcasing various configurations.
These stories provide visual representations and usage examples for different scenarios.
/
import { PrimaryStory, ButtonStory, CustomFormStory } from "./index";
export default {
title: "Elements/eox-feedback",
tags: ["autodocs"],
component: "eox-feedback",
};
/**
Primary story showcasing basic usage. The `endpoint` is set to a fake URL for demonstration purposes.

```html
<eox-feedback
  endpoint="/fake/endpoint"
  style="position: relative !important; height: 250px"
></eox-feedback>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/feedback/dist/eox-feedback.js";

  const eoxFeedback = document.querySelector("eox-feedback");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxFeedback, {
    unstyled: undefined,
  });

  // Listen for events
  eoxFeedback.addEventListener("submit", (e) =>
    console.log("Feedback submitted", e.detail),
  );
  eoxFeedback.addEventListener("close", () => console.log("Feedback closed"));
</script>

```

---

## eox-geosearch

`eox-geosearch` provides a flexible geocoding and location search interface using the OpenCage API.
It can be used standalone or integrated with `eox-map` for interactive map zooming and result visualization.

Main features:
- Search for locations using OpenCage API
- Integrate with `eox-map` for zooming to results
- Customizable loader SVG
- Geographic extent limiting
- Tooltip support
- Additional OpenCage API parameters via args
- Button mode for compact UI
- Flexible alignment and direction options

### Attributes

- `endpoint` (string): The OpenCage API endpoint to use for the search, including the key but without the query parameter.
- `for` (String|HTMLElement): Query selector of an `eox-map` (`String`, passed as an attribute or property)
or an `eox-map` DOM element (`HTMLElement`, passed as property)
- `query-parameter` (string): The name of the query parameter to use for the search query in the endpoint URI.
- `button` (boolean): Whether or not to enable button mode, which hides and shows the input field
similar to how a modal works.
- `interval` (number): Set a custom interval for the debounce function.
- `small` (boolean): Enables a smaller version of the button for use in map controls.
- `label` (string): Which text to use for the button if it is enabled.
- `list-direction` (string): The direction of the search input relative to the button, with the following options:

- `left`
- `top`
- `right`
- `bottom`
- `results-direction` (string): The direction of the results box relative to the input, with the following options:

- `left`
- `top`
- `right`
- `bottom`
- `unstyled` (boolean): Render the element without additional styles
- `loader-svg` (string): Svg used as loading indicator
- `extent` (string): Geographic extent to limit search results in the format "minLon,minLat,maxLon,maxLat".
This corresponds to OpenCage API's bounds parameter.
Example: "-0.563160,51.280430,0.278970,51.683979"
- `tooltip` (string): Tooltip text to display on the search button/input (uses BeerCSS tooltip).
- `tooltip-direction` (string): Direction of the tooltip relative to the button.
Options: "top", "bottom", "left", "right"
Default: "left"
- `params` (object): Additional parameters to add to the query string as key-value pairs.
Example: { language: "en", limit: 10, countrycode: "us" }

### Properties

- `endpoint` (string): The OpenCage API endpoint to use for the search, including the key but without the query parameter. (Default: `undefined`)
- `for` (String|HTMLElement): Query selector of an `eox-map` (`String`, passed as an attribute or property)
or an `eox-map` DOM element (`HTMLElement`, passed as property) (Default: `"eox-map"`)
- `queryParameter` (string): The name of the query parameter to use for the search query in the endpoint URI. (Default: `"q"`)
- `button` (boolean): Whether or not to enable button mode, which hides and shows the input field
similar to how a modal works. (Default: `false`)
- `label` (string): Which text to use for the button if it is enabled. (Default: `undefined`)
- `direction` (string): The direction of the search input relative to the button, with the following options:

- `left`
- `top`
- `right`
- `bottom` (Default: `"right"`)
- `resultsDirection` (string): The direction of the results box relative to the input, with the following options:

- `left`
- `top`
- `right`
- `bottom` (Default: `"down"`)
- `interval` (number): Set a custom interval for the debounce function. (Default: `800`)
- `small` (boolean): Enables a smaller version of the button for use in map controls. (Default: `false`)
- `unstyled` (boolean): Render the element without additional styles (Default: `false`)
- `loaderSvg` (string): Svg used as loading indicator (Default: `` <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 100 100" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid" width="50" height="50" style="shape-rendering: auto; display: block; background: transparent;" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" > <g> <circle stroke-dasharray="164.93361431346415 56.97787143782138" r="35" stroke-width="12" stroke="#1a467c" fill="none" cy="50" cx="50" > <animateTransform keyTimes="0;1" values="0 50 50;360 50 50" dur="1.2222222222222223s" repeatCount="indefinite" type="rotate" attributeName="transform" ></animateTransform> </circle> <g></g> </g> </svg> ``)
- `extent` (string): Geographic extent to limit search results in the format "minLon,minLat,maxLon,maxLat".
This corresponds to OpenCage API's bounds parameter.
Example: "-0.563160,51.280430,0.278970,51.683979" (Default: `undefined`)
- `tooltip` (string): Tooltip text to display on the search button/input (uses BeerCSS tooltip). (Default: `undefined`)
- `tooltipDirection` (string): Direction of the tooltip relative to the button.
Options: "top", "bottom", "left", "right"
Default: "left" (Default: `"left"`)
- `params` (object): Additional parameters to add to the query string as key-value pairs.
Example: { language: "en", limit: 10, countrycode: "us" } (Default: `undefined`)

### Events

- `geosearchSelect`: The select event, including the details of the selected item

### Examples

#### AdditionalParameters

Demonstrates passing additional parameters to the OpenCage API, such as language, result limit, and country code, all via the `params` property.
Note that the endpoint used here is a mock data file for demonstration purposes (it returns a few selected locations and is not a full geocoding service).
In order to use a real geocoding service, replace the endpoint with a valid OpenCage API URL along with your API key.

```html
<eox-geosearch
  endpoint="/opencage-mock-data.json"
  label="Search"
  style="position: absolute; top: 36px; left: 32px; z-index: 12"
  params='{"language":"en","limit":5,"countrycode":"us"}'
></eox-geosearch>
<eox-map
  id="geosearch-map-primary"
  style="width: 100%; height: 500px"
></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/geosearch/dist/eox-geosearch.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxGeosearch = document.querySelector("eox-geosearch");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    animationOptions: {
      duration: 400,
      padding: [10, 10, 10, 10],
    },
    config: {
      layers: [
        {
          type: "Tile",
          source: {
            type: "OSM",
          },
        },
      ],
      view: {
        center: [15, 48],
        zoom: 3,
      },
    },
  });
</script>

```

#### ButtonMode

Button mode hides the input field until the button is clicked. Useful for compact map controls.
Note that the endpoint used here is a mock data file for demonstration purposes (it returns a few selected locations and is not a full geocoding service).
In order to use a real geocoding service, replace the endpoint with a valid OpenCage API URL along with your API key.

```html
<eox-geosearch
  endpoint="/opencage-mock-data.json"
  label="Search"
  style="position: absolute; top: 36px; right: 32px; z-index: 12"
  small
  button
></eox-geosearch>
<eox-map
  id="geosearch-map-primary"
  style="width: 100%; height: 500px"
></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/geosearch/dist/eox-geosearch.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxGeosearch = document.querySelector("eox-geosearch");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxGeosearch, {
    listDirection: "left",
    resultsDirection: "down",
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    animationOptions: {
      duration: 400,
      padding: [10, 10, 10, 10],
    },
    config: {
      layers: [
        {
          type: "Tile",
          source: {
            type: "OSM",
          },
        },
      ],
      view: {
        center: [15, 48],
        zoom: 3,
      },
    },
  });
</script>

```

#### CustomAlignments

Demonstrates multiple eox-geosearch elements with different alignments and directions.
Note that the endpoint used here is a mock data file for demonstration purposes (it returns a few selected locations and is not a full geocoding service).
In order to use a real geocoding service, replace the endpoint with a valid OpenCage API URL along with your API key.

```html
<eox-geosearch
  endpoint="/opencage-mock-data.json"
  label="Search"
  style="position: absolute; z-index: 12"
  small
  button
></eox-geosearch>
<eox-map
  id="geosearch-map-primary"
  style="width: 100%; height: 600px"
></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/geosearch/dist/eox-geosearch.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxGeosearch = document.querySelector("eox-geosearch");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    animationOptions: {
      duration: 400,
      padding: [10, 10, 10, 10],
    },
    config: {
      layers: [
        {
          type: "Tile",
          source: {
            type: "OSM",
          },
        },
      ],
      view: {
        center: [15, 48],
        zoom: 3,
      },
    },
  });
</script>

```

#### CustomLoader

Shows how to use a custom SVG loader for the search input.
Note that the endpoint used here is a mock data file for demonstration purposes (it returns a few selected locations and is not a full geocoding service).
In order to use a real geocoding service, replace the endpoint with a valid OpenCage API URL along with your API key.

```html
<eox-geosearch
  endpoint="/opencage-mock-data.json"
  label="Search"
  style="position: absolute; top: 36px; left: 32px; z-index: 12"
></eox-geosearch>
<eox-map
  id="geosearch-map-primary"
  style="width: 100%; height: 500px"
></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/geosearch/dist/eox-geosearch.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxGeosearch = document.querySelector("eox-geosearch");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxGeosearch, {
    loaderSvg:
      '\n      <svg\n        xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"\n        width="48px"\n        height="48px"\n        viewBox="0 0 24 24"\n      >\n        <circle cx="12" cy="12" r="0" fill="currentColor">\n          <animate\n            id="svgSpinnersPulseMultiple0"\n            fill="freeze"\n            attributeName="r"\n            begin="0;svgSpinnersPulseMultiple2.end"\n            calcMode="spline"\n            dur="1.2s"\n            keySplines=".52,.6,.25,.99"\n            values="0;11"\n          />\n          <animate\n            fill="freeze"\n            attributeName="opacity"\n            begin="0;svgSpinnersPulseMultiple2.end"\n            calcMode="spline"\n            dur="1.2s"\n            keySplines=".52,.6,.25,.99"\n            values="1;0"\n          />\n        </circle>\n        <circle cx="12" cy="12" r="0" fill="currentColor">\n          <animate\n            id="svgSpinnersPulseMultiple1"\n            fill="freeze"\n            attributeName="r"\n            begin="svgSpinnersPulseMultiple0.begin+0.2s"\n            calcMode="spline"\n            dur="1.2s"\n            keySplines=".52,.6,.25,.99"\n            values="0;11"\n          />\n          <animate\n            fill="freeze"\n            attributeName="opacity"\n            begin="svgSpinnersPulseMultiple0.begin+0.2s"\n            calcMode="spline"\n            dur="1.2s"\n            keySplines=".52,.6,.25,.99"\n            values="1;0"\n          />\n        </circle>\n        <circle cx="12" cy="12" r="0" fill="currentColor">\n          <animate\n            id="svgSpinnersPulseMultiple2"\n            fill="freeze"\n            attributeName="r"\n            begin="svgSpinnersPulseMultiple0.begin+0.4s"\n            calcMode="spline"\n            dur="1.2s"\n            keySplines=".52,.6,.25,.99"\n            values="0;11"\n          />\n          <animate\n            fill="freeze"\n            attributeName="opacity"\n            begin="svgSpinnersPulseMultiple0.begin+0.4s"\n            calcMode="spline"\n            dur="1.2s"\n            keySplines=".52,.6,.25,.99"\n            values="1;0"\n          />\n        </circle>\n      </svg>\n    ',
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    animationOptions: {
      duration: 400,
      padding: [10, 10, 10, 10],
    },
    config: {
      layers: [
        {
          type: "Tile",
          source: {
            type: "OSM",
          },
        },
      ],
      view: {
        center: [15, 48],
        zoom: 3,
      },
    },
  });
</script>

```

#### ExtentLimit

Limits search results to a specified geographic extent. Tooltip and direction are also customizable via attributes.
Note that the endpoint used here is a mock data file for demonstration purposes (it returns a few selected locations and is not a full geocoding service).
In order to use a real geocoding service, replace the endpoint with a valid OpenCage API URL along with your API key.

```html
<eox-geosearch
  endpoint="/opencage-mock-data.json"
  label="Search"
  style="position: absolute; top: 36px; left: 32px; z-index: 12"
  button
  small
  extent="-125.0,24.0,-66.0,49.0"
  tooltip="Search within North America"
></eox-geosearch>
<eox-map id="geosearch-map-extent" style="width: 100%; height: 500px"></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/geosearch/dist/eox-geosearch.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxGeosearch = document.querySelector("eox-geosearch");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxGeosearch, {
    tooltipDirection: "right",
  });

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    animationOptions: {
      duration: 400,
      padding: [10, 10, 10, 10],
    },
    config: {
      layers: [
        {
          type: "Tile",
          source: {
            type: "OSM",
          },
        },
      ],
      view: {
        center: [-95, 38],
        zoom: 4,
      },
    },
  });
</script>

```

#### Primary

Basic usage of eox-geosearch with a default OpenCage endpoint. The search bar is positioned on the map and interacts with the map to zoom to selected results.
Note that the endpoint used here is a mock data file for demonstration purposes (it returns a few selected locations and is not a full geocoding service).
In order to use a real geocoding service, replace the endpoint with a valid OpenCage API URL along with your API key.

```html
<eox-geosearch
  endpoint="/opencage-mock-data.json"
  label="Search"
  style="position: absolute; top: 36px; left: 32px; z-index: 12"
></eox-geosearch>
<eox-map
  id="geosearch-map-primary"
  style="width: 100%; height: 500px"
></eox-map>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/geosearch/dist/eox-geosearch.js";
  import "@eox/map/dist/eox-map.js";

  const eoxGeosearch = document.querySelector("eox-geosearch");
  const eoxMap = document.querySelector("eox-map");

  Object.assign(eoxMap, {
    animationOptions: {
      duration: 400,
      padding: [10, 10, 10, 10],
    },
    config: {
      layers: [
        {
          type: "Tile",
          source: {
            type: "OSM",
          },
        },
      ],
      view: {
        center: [15, 48],
        zoom: 3,
      },
    },
  });
</script>

```

---

## eox-itemfilter

The `eox-itemfilter` element provides a comprehensive item filtering system for lists of items with flexible filter types, result aggregation, and customizable display modes.

Features:
- Supports `text`, `select`, `multiselect`, `range` (including date), and `spatial` filters
- Aggregates results by a property key (e.g. themes)
- Inline mode for compact UI
- Pre-set filter state via `state` in `filterProperties` property/attribute
- Nested property filtering using dot notation
- External search API endpoint support via `externalFilter` property
- Auto-spreading of single-item aggregations to root level
- Card display mode for results (`result-type="cards"`)
- Secondary result action button with custom icon and event handler (`click:result-action`)
- Custom result sorting via `resultSorting` property/attribute
- Customizable layout and styling via CSS variables

Usage examples and visual demos are available in Storybook stories, including scenarios for inline mode, external filtering, nested properties, card display, CSS variable customization, and result actions.

### Attributes

- `items` (Array<Object>): The items to be filtered. Can be passed as a property or stringified JSON attribute.
- `selectedResult` (Object): The currently selected result
- `aggregate-results` (String): Aggregate results by a property key
- `auto-spread-single` (Boolean): Automatically spread single item summaries
removing the summary header
- `enable-highlighting` (Boolean): Highlighting of search result character matches
- `filterProperties` (Array<{key?: String, keys?: Array<String>, title: String, type: String, placeholder?: String, format?: String, state?: Object}>): The filter properties. Can be passed as a property or stringified JSON attribute.
- `fuseConfig` (Object): Native fuse.js config override
- `inline-mode` (Boolean): Inline mode, for rendering the itemfilter in a very condensed space.
Expects showResults to be false
- `match-all-when-empty` (Boolean): Show all result items if nothing is input by the user
- `show-result` (Boolean): Display results list
- `id-property` (String): Unique id property of items
- `title-property` (String|Function): The property of the result items used for display
Supports passing a function which recieves the current item as parameter and is expected to return a string
- `sub-title-property` (String|Function): The property of the result items used for a subtitle
Supports passing a function which recieves the current item as parameter and is expected to return a string
- `image-property` (String|Function): The property of the result items used for an image
Supports passing a function which recieves the current item as parameter and is expected to return a string
- `resultSorting` (false|string|Function|{key:string, order?:'asc'|'desc'}): Sorting behavior for the results. Can be:
- undefined (default): smart alphabetical sorting (skips if externalFilter or fuseConfig.shouldSort is truthy)
- false: no sorting
- string: property key to sort by (ascending)
- function: custom comparator function (a, b) => number
- object: { key: string, order?: 'asc' | 'desc' }
- `enable-multiple-filter` (Boolean): Allow opening multiple filter accordions in parallel
- `expand-result` (Boolean): Initialize result accordions expanded
- `expand-multiple-results` (Boolean): Allow opening multiple result accordions in parallel
- `result-type` (String): Rendering type for results. Can be `list`or `cards` (`cards` requires also importing the `eox-layout` element)
- `enableResultAction` (Boolean): Enable result action button (a secondary action besides the normal result selection)
- `resultActionIcon` (String): Icon for result action. Supports html
- `styleOverride` (String): Overrides elements current CSS.
- `unstyled` (boolean): Render the element without additional styles

### Properties

- `items` (Array<Object>): The items to be filtered. Can be passed as a property or stringified JSON attribute. (Default: `null`)
- `filters` (Object): The state object containing the applied filters. (Default: `{}`)
- `results` (Array<Object>): The state object containing the filtered results. (Default: `[]`)
- `selectedResult` (Object): The currently selected result (Default: `null`)
- `aggregateResults` (String): Aggregate results by a property key (Default: `undefined`)
- `autoSpreadSingle` (Boolean): Automatically spread single item summaries
removing the summary header (Default: `false`)
- `enableHighlighting` (Boolean): Highlighting of search result character matches (Default: `false`)
- `externalFilter` (() => string| (() => {url:string;key?:string;fetchFn?:string})): Use an external search endpoint instead of fuse search.
Passed properties: input string, filters object.
The function can return a URL string, or an object containing a `url,
an optional `fetchFn` to handle the request, or/and an optional `key` to
return a specific property from the fetched data.
This is a property-only field as it expects a function. (Default: `null`)
- `filterProperties` (Array<{key?: String, keys?: Array<String>, title: String, type: String, placeholder?: String, format?: String, state?: Object}>): The filter properties. Can be passed as a property or stringified JSON attribute. (Default: `[]`)
- `fuseConfig` (Object): Native fuse.js config override (Default: `{}`)
- `inlineMode` (Boolean): Inline mode, for rendering the itemfilter in a very condensed space.
Expects showResults to be false (Default: `false`)
- `matchAllWhenEmpty` (Boolean): Show all result items if nothing is input by the user (Default: `true`)
- `showResults` (Boolean): Display results list (Default: `true`)
- `titleProperty` (String|Function): The property of the result items used for display
Supports passing a function which recieves the current item as parameter and is expected to return a string (Default: `"title"`)
- `subTitleProperty` (String|Function): The property of the result items used for a subtitle
Supports passing a function which recieves the current item as parameter and is expected to return a string (Default: `undefined`)
- `imageProperty` (String|Function): The property of the result items used for an image
Supports passing a function which recieves the current item as parameter and is expected to return a string (Default: `undefined`)
- `resultSorting` (false|string|Function|{key:string, order?:'asc'|'desc'}): Sorting behavior for the results. Can be:
- undefined (default): smart alphabetical sorting (skips if externalFilter or fuseConfig.shouldSort is truthy)
- false: no sorting
- string: property key to sort by (ascending)
- function: custom comparator function (a, b) => number
- object: { key: string, order?: 'asc' | 'desc' } (Default: `undefined`)
- `idProperty` (String): Unique id property of items (Default: `"id"`)
- `expandMultipleFilters` (Boolean): Allow opening multiple filter accordions in parallel (Default: `true`)
- `expandResults` (Boolean): Initialize result accordions expanded (Default: `true`)
- `expandMultipleResults` (Boolean): Allow opening multiple result accordions in parallel (Default: `true`)
- `resultType` (String): Rendering type for results. Can be `list`or `cards` (`cards` requires also importing the `eox-layout` element) (Default: `"list"`)
- `enableResultAction` (Boolean): Enable result action button (a secondary action besides the normal result selection) (Default: `false`)
- `resultActionIcon` (String): Icon for result action. Supports html (Default: `'<svg style="width: 24px;" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><title>click me!</title><path fill="currentColor" d="M11.5,11L17.88,16.37L17,16.55L16.36,16.67C15.73,16.8 15.37,17.5 15.65,18.07L15.92,18.65L17.28,21.59L15.86,22.25L14.5,19.32L14.24,18.74C13.97,18.15 13.22,17.97 12.72,18.38L12.21,18.78L11.5,19.35V11M10.76,8.69A0.76,0.76 0 0,0 10,9.45V20.9C10,21.32 10.34,21.66 10.76,21.66C10.95,21.66 11.11,21.6 11.24,21.5L13.15,19.95L14.81,23.57C14.94,23.84 15.21,24 15.5,24C15.61,24 15.72,24 15.83,23.92L18.59,22.64C18.97,22.46 19.15,22 18.95,21.63L17.28,18L19.69,17.55C19.85,17.5 20,17.43 20.12,17.29C20.39,16.97 20.35,16.5 20,16.21L11.26,8.86L11.25,8.87C11.12,8.76 10.95,8.69 10.76,8.69M15,10V8H20V10H15M13.83,4.76L16.66,1.93L18.07,3.34L15.24,6.17L13.83,4.76M10,0H12V5H10V0M3.93,14.66L6.76,11.83L8.17,13.24L5.34,16.07L3.93,14.66M3.93,3.34L5.34,1.93L8.17,4.76L6.76,6.17L3.93,3.34M7,10H2V8H7V10" /></svg>'`)
- `styleOverride` (String): Overrides elements current CSS. (Default: `""`)
- `unstyled` (boolean): Render the element without additional styles (Default: `false`)

### Events

- `filter`: When filters are applied, with `results` and `filters` in event detail
- `select`: When a result is selected, with `selectedResult` in event detail
- `mouseenter:result`: When a result is hovered
- `mouseleave:result`: When a result is un-hovered
- `click:result-action`: When a result action button is clicked

### Examples

#### AutoSpread

Use `autoSpreadSingle` to automatically spread single-item aggregations to the root level, simplifying the result view when only one item matches a filter.

```html
<eox-itemfilter></eox-itemfilter>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/itemfilter/dist/eox-itemfilter.js";

  const eoxItemfilter = document.querySelector("eox-itemfilter");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxItemfilter, {
    titleProperty: "title",
    aggregateResults: "themes",
    autoSpreadSingle: true,
    filterProperties: [
      {
        key: "themes",
        title: "Theme",
        type: "select",
        placeholder: "Select a theme",
        expanded: true,
        inline: false,
        state: {
          air: true,
        },
      },
    ],
    items: [
      {
        archived: false,
        code: "E10a2",
        description: "Actively managed total area from Sentinel-2 data",
        indicator: "Actively managed total area from Sentinel-2 data",
        themes: ["agriculture"],
        title: "White Asparagus area [%]",
        name: "Actively managed total area from Sentinel-2 data",
        year: 2000,
        likes: 4,
        years: [2000, 2000],
        correlation: 0.3,
        rating: 20,
        timestamp: "2023-06-14T13:56:38+00:00",
        datetime: ["2023-06-14T10:56:38+00:00", "2023-06-14T22:56:38+00:00"],
        bbox: [-90, 60, -20, 82],
        geometry: {
          type: "Polygon",
          coordinates: [
            [
              [-90, 60],
              [-20, 60],
              [-20, 82],
              [-90, 82],
            ],
          ],
        },
        tags: ["agriculture", "Sentinel-2", "asparagus"],
        status: {
          code: "active",
        },
        assets: {
          thumbnail: {
            href: "https://picsum.photos/800/600?random=1",
          },
        },
        properties: {
          started: "2023-06-14T13:56:38+00:00",
          geometry: {
            type: "Polygon",
            coordinates: [
              [
                [-90, 60],
                [-20, 60],
                [-20, 82],
                [-90, 82],
              ],
            ],
          },
        },
      },
      {
        archived: false,
        code: "E10a9",
        description: "Agricultural Workers",
        indicator: "Agricultural Workers",
        themes: ["agriculture"],
        title: "Workers availability [nr]",
        name: "Agricultural Workers",
        year: 2020,
        likes: 46,
        years: [2007, 2020],
        correlation: 0.3,
        rating: 10,
        timestamp: "2023-06-13T13:56:38+00:00",
        datetime: ["2023-06-13T10:56:38+00:00", "2023-06-13T22:56:38+00:00"],
        bbox: [0, 0, 10, 10],
        geometry: {
          type: "Polygon",
          coordinates: [
            [
              [0, 0],
              [10, 0],
              [10, 10],
              [0, 10],
            ],
          ],
        },
        tags: ["agriculture", "workers", "availability"],
        status: {
          code: "inactive",
        },
        properties: {
          started: "2023-06-13T13:56:38+00:00",
          geometry: {
            type: "Polygon",
            coordinates: [
              [
                [0, 0],
                [10, 0],
                [10, 10],
                [0, 10],
              ],
            ],
          },
        },
      },
      {
        archived: false,
        code: "N1",
        description: "Air Quality",
        indicator: "Air Quality",
        themes: ["air"],
        title: "Sea ice freeboard",
        name: "Sea ice freeboard",
        year: 2023,
        likes: 34,
        years: [2008, 2023],
        correlation: -0.4,
        rating: -30,
        timestamp: "2023-06-12T13:56:38+00:00",
        datetime: ["2023-06-12T10:56:38+00:00", "2023-06-12T22:56:38+00:00"],
        bbox: [-180, -80, 180, -61],
        geometry: {
          type: "Polygon",
          coordinates: [
            [
              [-180, -80],
              [180, -80],
              [180, -61],
              [-180, -61],
            ],
          ],
        },
        tags: ["air quality", "sea ice", "freeboard"],
        status: {
          code: "active",
        },
        assets: {
          thumbnail: {
            href: "https://picsum.photos/800/600?random=3",
          },
        },
        properties: {
          started: "2023-06-12T13:56:38+00:00",
          geometry: {
            type: "Polygon",
            coordinates: [
              [
                [-180, -80],
                [180, -80],
                [180, -61],
                [-180, -61],
              ],
            ],
          },
        },
      },
      {
        archived: false,
        code: "E13o",
        description:
          "Average number of vessels in a km2 cell in a given period",
        indicator: "Average number of vessels in a km2 cell in a given period",
        themes: ["economy"],
        title: "Vessel density (all) [h/sqkm]",
        name: "Average number of vessels in a km2 cell in a given period",
        year: 2022,
        likes: 177,
        years: [2021, 2022],
        correlation: 0.3,
        rating: -50,
        timestamp: "2023-06-11T13:56:38+00:00",
        datetime: ["2023-06-11T10:56:38+00:00", "2023-06-11T22:56:38+00:00"],
        tags: ["economy", "vessel density", "average"],
        status: {
          code: "inactive",
        },
        assets: {
          thumbnail: {
            href: "https://picsum.photos/800/600?random=4",
          },

```

#### CSSVariables

CSS variables can be used to modify the styling and layout of itemfilter: e.g. the `--form-flex-direction`
variable set to `row` instead of `column` (default) for a horizontal layout.

```html
<eox-itemfilter style="--form-flex-direction: row"></eox-itemfilter>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/itemfilter/dist/eox-itemfilter.js";

  const eoxItemfilter = document.querySelector("eox-itemfilter");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxItemfilter, {
    aggregateResults: "themes",
    titleProperty: "title",
    subTitleProperty: "description",
    filterProperties: [
      {
        key: "themes",
        title: "Theme",
        type: "multiselect",
      },
    ],
    items: [
      {
        archived: false,
        code: "E10a2",
        description: "Actively managed total area from Sentinel-2 data",
        indicator: "Actively managed total area from Sentinel-2 data",
        themes: ["agriculture"],
        title: "White Asparagus area [%]",
        name: "Actively managed total area from Sentinel-2 data",
        year: 2000,
        likes: 4,
        years: [2000, 2000],
        correlation: 0.3,
        rating: 20,
        timestamp: "2023-06-14T13:56:38+00:00",
        datetime: ["2023-06-14T10:56:38+00:00", "2023-06-14T22:56:38+00:00"],
        bbox: [-90, 60, -20, 82],
        geometry: {
          type: "Polygon",
          coordinates: [
            [
              [-90, 60],
              [-20, 60],
              [-20, 82],
              [-90, 82],
            ],
          ],
        },
        tags: ["agriculture", "Sentinel-2", "asparagus"],
        status: {
          code: "active",
        },
        assets: {
          thumbnail: {
            href: "https://picsum.photos/800/600?random=1",
          },
        },
        properties: {
          started: "2023-06-14T13:56:38+00:00",
          geometry: {
            type: "Polygon",
            coordinates: [
              [
                [-90, 60],
                [-20, 60],
                [-20, 82],
                [-90, 82],
              ],
            ],
          },
        },
      },
      {
        archived: false,
        code: "E10a9",
        description: "Agricultural Workers",
        indicator: "Agricultural Workers",
        themes: ["agriculture"],
        title: "Workers availability [nr]",
        name: "Agricultural Workers",
        year: 2020,
        likes: 46,
        years: [2007, 2020],
        correlation: 0.3,
        rating: 10,
        timestamp: "2023-06-13T13:56:38+00:00",
        datetime: ["2023-06-13T10:56:38+00:00", "2023-06-13T22:56:38+00:00"],
        bbox: [0, 0, 10, 10],
        geometry: {
          type: "Polygon",
          coordinates: [
            [
              [0, 0],
              [10, 0],
              [10, 10],
              [0, 10],
            ],
          ],
        },

        status: {

```

#### CardDisplay

Render results in a responsive card grid using the `result-type` property set to `cards`. Ideal for visual browsing and highlighting item images and details.
Requires to also import `@eox/layout` for card styling.

```html
<eox-itemfilter
  style="
    height: 500px;
    --card-gap: 16px;
    --card-width: 300px;
    --card-height: 200px;
    --card-border-radius: 8px;
    --form-flex-direction: row;
    --card-title-font: Arial, sans-serif;
    --card-transition: all 0.35s ease;
    --card-hover-transform: translateY(-10px);
    --card-box-shadow: 0 10px 20px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
    --padding: 40px;
    --padding-vertical: 40px;
  "
></eox-itemfilter>

<script type="module">
  import "@eox/itemfilter/dist/eox-itemfilter.js";

  import "@eox/layout


  const eoxItemfilter = document.querySelector("eox-itemfilter");

  // Assign properties
  Object.assign(eoxItemfilter, {
    aggregateResults: "themes",
      titleProperty: "title",
      subTitleProperty: "description",
      imageProperty: "assets.thumbnail.href",
      resultType: "cards",
      filterProperties: [
    {
      "key": "themes",
      "title": "Theme",
      "type": "multiselect"
    }
  ],
      items: [
    {
      "archived": false,
      "code": "E10a2",
      "description": "Actively managed total area from Sentinel-2 data",
      "indicator": "Actively managed total area from Sentinel-2 data",
      "themes": [
        "agriculture"
      ],
      "title": "White Asparagus area [%]",
      "name": "Actively managed total area from Sentinel-2 data",
      "year": 2000,
      "likes": 4,
      "years": [
        2000,
        2000
      ],
      "correlation": 0.3,
      "rating": 20,
      "timestamp": "2023-06-14T13:56:38+00:00",
      "datetime": [
        "2023-06-14T10:56:38+00:00",
        "2023-06-14T22:56:38+00:00"
      ],
      "bbox": [
        -90,
        60,
        -20,
        82
      ],
      "geometry": {
        "type": "Polygon",
        "coordinates": [
          [
            [
              -90,

            [
              -20,
```
